Off Calendar Oct-12-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
AMERICAN ZURICH INSURANCE COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.

APPEARANCES: Vivian Leung Lerche, Esq. of Law Offices of Bishop Barry Drath 510/596-0888 for moving party American Zurich Insurance Company; Michelle M. Meyers, Esq. of Burnham Brown 510/835-6703 for defts/x-complainants & opposing parties.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - Plaintiff's motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternative motion for summary adjudication is granted as to the sixth, eighth, ninth and thirteenth causes of action in the second amended cross-complaint filed by Sanact and Mr. Wray. Summary judgment is improper because the motion does not disposes of all the claims between the parties. Summary adjudication is proper because paragraph 7 of the cross-complaint alleges that a true and correct copy of the 99 policy is attached to the cross-complaint. The 99 policy includes the form 10 endorsement. Even thought the endorsement is not signed or the blanks filled in, the endorsement is still part of the policy. The endorsement precludes coverage for Mr. Simmons' workers' compensation case. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077
